⚠️ Important Disclaimer

This tool (Attendance Register Pro) is a proprietary internal compliance aid developed exclusively for use by clients and staff of Advocate R.K. Bhandari & Associates. It is intended solely for administrative and record-keeping assistance.

NOT FOR LEGAL USE: The attendance records, reports, and data generated by this tool do not constitute legal evidence, certified statutory documents, or official records under the Factories Act 1948, Shops & Establishments Act, ESI Act 1948, EPF Act 1952, or any other applicable law, unless separately verified, certified, and authenticated by the authorised signatory of the establishment.

NO LIABILITY: Advocate R.K. Bhandari & Associates and its affiliates shall not be liable for any statutory penalty, assessment, or legal consequence arising from reliance on this tool's output without independent verification.

DATA PRIVACY: This tool operates entirely within your browser. No employee data is transmitted to or stored on any external server. All processing is local to your device.

RESTRICTED USE: Unauthorised reproduction, redistribution, or commercial use of this tool is strictly prohibited and may attract legal action under applicable intellectual property laws.

A — Absent
OFF — Weekly Off
EL — Earned Leave

Modified cell
📋 How To Use This Tool
  • Prepare an Excel file (.xlsx) with headers: Employee Name and NCP AVAILED. Each row = one employee.
  • Fill in Establishment Details: ESI Code, EPF Code, Shift Timing, Month, and Weekly Off day in the configuration section.
  • Click Upload Excel to load your employee data. The tool will auto-generate attendance with OFF days marked.
  • NCP (Non-Contributing Period) absences are distributed randomly across the month. You can manually edit any cell.
  • Right-click (or click) any attendance cell to change the status: P / A / OFF / EL / CL / H / ML / LWP.
  • Modified cells are highlighted in dark orange for easy identification.
  • Use the Search bar at the top toolbar or the floating search button (bottom-right) to filter employees.
  • Click Download PDF to generate a print-ready Muster Roll. 20 employees per page.
  • Click Export Excel to download attendance data as a spreadsheet.
  • Click Reset to regenerate attendance from original uploaded data.
⚙️ Automatic Rules Explained
  • Weekly Off → Absent Rule: If an employee is absent for 3 or more days in a week (Monday to Sunday), the weekly OFF of that week is automatically converted to A (Absent). This is based on the legal principle under the Factories Act and Shops Act — that an employee forfeits the right to a paid weekly off if absent on both the preceding and succeeding working days.
  • You can change the threshold (2 or 3 absents) or disable this rule from the "Absent → OFF Rule" dropdown in Establishment Details.
  • The Paid Days column (rightmost) = P + OFF + H + EL + CL + ML. LWP days are excluded from paid count.
  • Days beyond the month's actual working days are shown as — (e.g., 29/30/31 in a 28-day month).
  • Holidays are counted as paid days. If an employee was absent on the day before and after a holiday, consider marking it as LWP.
⚖️ Statutory Compliance Reference
Provision Act / Section Requirement
Muster Roll Maintenance Factories Act Sec. 62 Every factory shall maintain a register of adult workers in Form No. 12. Attendance must be recorded daily before the shift begins. This register must be preserved for 3 years.
Muster Roll — Shops Act Punjab Shops Act Sec. 14A Every establishment shall keep a muster roll showing name, hours worked, and attendance for all employees. Register must be available for inspection.
Weekly Rest Factories Act Sec. 52 No adult worker shall work for more than 10 days continuously without a rest day. Weekly off can be shifted by substitution (Sec. 52(3)) with prior notice to Inspector.
Weekly Rest — Shops Punjab Shops Act Sec. 9 Every employee shall be granted one holiday per week. The employer must display the weekly off day on the notice board.
Weekly Off Forfeiture Factories Act Sec. 52 r/w Wages Act If a worker is absent without authorised leave on the day before and the day after a rest day, the employer may refuse to pay wages for the rest day. This is the legal basis for the "3 absents" rule in this tool.
Earned Leave Factories Act Sec. 79 Worker completing 240 days of work in a calendar year is entitled to 1 day EL for every 20 days worked (factories). Carry forward of unused EL up to 30 days permitted.
Earned Leave — Shops Punjab Shops Act Sec. 32 Employee entitled to 1 day EL for every 20 days of work after completing 12 months of continuous service. Maximum accumulation: 60 days.
ESI Attendance Impact ESI Act Sec. 2(22) Wages for purposes of ESI contribution include payment for days not worked (leave with pay, holidays). NCP days reduce the contribution period accordingly.
EPF Attendance Impact EPF Act Para 38(3) Employer must submit attendance details with monthly ECR. NCP days in ECR must match muster roll absences. Discrepancy can attract assessment proceedings.
Maternity Leave Maternity Benefit Act Sec. 5 26 weeks maternity leave (for first 2 children). 12 weeks for 3rd child onwards. To be marked as ML with full wages. Cannot be counted as absent or LWP.
Record Preservation Factories Act Rule (Punjab) Sec. 120 Muster Roll, Wage Register and all statutory registers to be preserved for minimum 3 years and produced on demand by Inspector of Factories / Labour Officer.
⚠️ Important Notes & Disclaimer
  • This tool is a compliance aid — final attendance records must be verified and certified by the authorised signatory before use in any statutory proceeding.
  • Always cross-reference NCP figures with biometric/swipe data before uploading.
  • This is a proprietary tool of Advocate R.K. Bhandari & Associates. Not for redistribution.
  • Laws referenced: Factories Act 1948, Punjab Shops & Commercial Establishments Act 1958, ESI Act 1948, EPF Act 1952, Maternity Benefit Act 1961, Contract Labour Act 1970, Payment of Wages Act 1936.
  • This tool stores no data on any server. All processing is done entirely in your browser.
